0.original: But like all progressive companies, we need new products, new markets, new and more efficient ways of doing things. 2 00:00:07,760 --> 00:00:10,210 We depend on people with initiative. 3 00:00:10,401 --> 00:00:13,171 They're the ball carriers on our team. 4 00:00:14,160 --> 00:00:16,931 Initiative is a special kind of action. 5 00:00:17,120 --> 00:00:21,011 It's doing something worthwhile without being told to do it. 6 00:00:21,361 --> 00:00:29,250 The person with initiative has a standing invitation to join the high-income brackets in every business and profession. 7 00:00:29,921 --> 00:00:38,850 The Director of Marketing Research in a medium-sized drug manufacturing firm told me how he got to be a Director of Marketing Research. 8 00:00:38,960 --> 00:00:41,891 It's a good lesson in the power of initiative. 9 00:00:42,560 --> 00:00:45,730 Five years ago, I got an idea, he told me. 10 00:00:45,921 --> 00:00:50,770 I was working then as a sort of missionary salesman, calling on wholesalers. 11 00:00:50,960 --> 00:00:57,330 I discovered that one thing we lacked was facts about the consumers we wanted to buy our drug line. 12 00:00:57,600 --> 00:01:01,330 I talked about the need for market research to everyone here. 13 00:01:01,680 --> 00:01:06,691 At first, I got only deaf ears because management couldn't see the need for it. 14 00:01:07,200 --> 00:01:11,810 I was pretty much obsessed with the idea of marketing research in our company, 15 00:01:12,000 --> 00:01:14,531 so I sort of took the bull by the horns. 16 00:01:14,720 --> 00:01:20,531 I asked and got permission to prepare a monthly report on facts of drug marketing. 17 00:01:20,720 --> 00:01:24,210 I collected information from every source I could find. 18 00:01:24,401 --> 00:01:32,451 I kept on with this, and pretty soon management and the other salesmen found themselves really interested in what I was doing. 19 00:01:32,720 --> 00:01:42,611 Just one year after I started crusading for research, I was relieved of my regular duties and asked to concentrate on developing my ideas. 20 00:01:43,361 --> 00:01:47,171 The rest, he continued, was just natural development. 21 00:01:47,361 --> 00:01:54,611 Now I've got two assistants, a secretary, and about three times the yearly income I had five years ago. 22 00:01:56,080 --> 00:02:01,091 Here are two special exercises for developing the initiative habit. 23 00:02:01,091 --> 00:02:02,691 1. 24 00:02:02,881 --> 00:02:04,370 Be a Crusader. 25 00:02:04,480 --> 00:02:09,330 When you see something that you believe ought to be done, pick up the ball and run. 26 00:02:09,921 --> 00:02:17,651 A new subdivision not far from where I live was about two-thirds built when expansion came almost to a standstill. 27 00:02:17,921 --> 00:02:21,730 A few families with a don't-care attitude had moved in. 28 00:02:22,080 --> 00:02:27,546 This prompted several of the finest families in the area to sell their homes at a loss and move on.
0.b1: But like all growing companies, we need new products, new markets, and better ways to work.
0.cn: 但像所有不断进步的公司一样，我们需要新产品、新市场以及更高效的工作方式。
0.ai_notes:
'progressive': describing a company that is modern, forward-thinking, and always looking for ways to improve.
'initiative': the ability to assess and start things independently without needing to be told what to do.
'ball carriers': a metaphor from American football, referring to people who take responsibility and move a project forward.
'worthwhile': something that is important or useful enough to spend time and effort on.
'high-income brackets': the group of people who earn the highest salaries in a society.
'missionary salesman': a salesperson who promotes a product or idea to create demand rather than just taking orders.
'calling on': a professional way to say visiting clients or customers to sell products.
'drug line': a specific collection or series of pharmaceutical products sold by a company.
'deaf ears': an idiom meaning people are ignoring your suggestions or refusing to listen.
'took the bull by the horns': an idiom meaning to deal with a difficult situation directly and with confidence.
'crusading': fighting strongly and with great passion for a cause or a change you believe in.
'relieved of': to be officially freed from a previous job or responsibility.
'subdivision': a piece of land divided into smaller lots for building houses.
'standstill': a situation where all activity or progress has completely stopped.
'don't-care attitude': a lack of interest or responsibility regarding the state of one's environment or community.
'at a loss': selling something for less money than you originally paid for it.
0.remarks:
'progressive': 形容一家公司现代、有前瞻性，并不断寻求改进。
'initiative': 指无需他人告知，能够独立评估并开展工作的能力。
'ball carriers': 源自美式橄榄球的隐喻，指那些承担责任并推动项目前进的人。
'worthwhile': 值得花时间和精力去做的事。
'high-income brackets': 社会中收入最高的群体。
'missionary salesman': 指通过推广产品或理念来创造需求，而非仅仅接受订单的销售人员。
'calling on': 拜访客户以推销产品的专业说法。
'drug line': 公司销售的特定药品系列。
'deaf ears': 习语，指人们无视你的建议或拒绝倾听。
'took the bull by the horns': 习语，指直接且自信地处理困难局面。
'crusading': 为自己信仰的事业或变革进行强烈且充满激情的斗争。
'relieved of': 被正式免除之前的工作或职责。
'subdivision': 为建造房屋而划分成小地块的土地。
'standstill': 所有活动或进展完全停止的状态。
'don't-care attitude': 对环境或社区状况缺乏兴趣或责任感。
'at a loss': 以低于原价的价格出售某物。
